description_long: This house features a simple, rectangular structure with a beige stucco exterior and a prominent red-tiled gable roof. The front facade displays a symmetrical arrangement of green shuttered windows and a small balcony with a metal railing above the main entrance. The roof includes a single small chimney and shows realistic weathering and texture. The side and rear walls are plain, with no visible windows or doors, emphasizing the utilitarian design. The building appears to be three stories tall, with each level marked by a row of windows. The overall style suggests a Mediterranean or Southern European influence, commonly found in urban neighborhoods.
description: A three-story beige stucco house with a red-tiled gable roof, green shuttered windows, and a small balcony above the main entrance.
description_view_0: Front view showing symmetrical green shuttered windows, a small balcony, and the main entrance.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the plain wall and the sloped red-tiled roof with a visible chimney.
description_view_2: Rear view with a featureless beige wall and the full expanse of the red-tiled roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the plain wall and roof slope.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry with a small balcony above, metal railing, and green shuttered windows; no porch or decorative columns.
